feed.title: Garlic Soffritto
feed.subtitle: Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.

entry.title: Crispy Arancini (Sicilian Stuffed Rice Balls)
entry.content:
Golden, crispy, and filled with savoury ragu and gooey mozzarella — arancini are the ultimate Sicilian street food snack.

📝 Ingredients

For the Rice:

  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 small onion, diced
  • 300g (1½ cups) arborio or carnaroli rice
  • 750ml (3 cups) chicken or vegetable broth
  • Pinch of saffron (optional)
  • 50g (¼ cup) grated Parmigiano
  • Salt to taste

For the Filling:

  • 150g (5 oz) meat ragù (leftover works great)
  • 80g (3 oz) mozzarella, diced

For Breading:

  • Flour
  • 2 eggs, beaten
  • Breadcrumbs
  • Vegetable oil (for frying)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Step 1: Cook the Rice

  1. In a saucepan, sauté onion in olive oil until soft.
  2. Add rice and toast for 2 minutes.
  3. Slowly add hot broth, stirring, until rice is tender and sticky.
  4. Stir in saffron (if using), Parmigiano, and salt. Let cool completely.

Step 2: Assemble the Arancini

  1. With damp hands, take a spoonful of rice and flatten it.
  2. Place a bit of ragù and mozzarella in the centre.
  3. Enclose filling with more rice and shape into a ball.

Step 3: Bread and Fry

  1. Roll each ball in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
  2. Heat oil to 170°C / 340°F. Fry until golden and crispy (about 3–4 mins each).
  3. Drain on paper towels.

🍷 Serving Suggestion

Serve warm as a snack or appetiser with a glass of Nero d’Avola or a chilled Rosé.

🌟 Chef’s Tip

Chill the shaped arancini before frying — they’ll hold together better and fry more evenly.

Buon appetito! 🧆
Show us your arancini! Tag @garlicsoffritto.
entry.summary: Golden, crispy, and filled with savoury ragu and gooey mozzarella — arancini are the ultimate Sicilian street food snack.

entry.title: Authentic Carbonara Sauce (Spaghetti alla Carbonara)
entry.content:
A true Roman classic — creamy without cream, rich with egg, pecorino, and guanciale. Carbonara is the soul of simplicity and indulgence.

📝 Ingredients

  • 400g (14 oz) spaghetti
  • 150g (5 oz) guanciale (or pancetta), cut into strips
  • 3 large egg yolks + 1 whole egg
  • 50g (½ cup) grated Pecorino Romano cheese
  • Freshly ground black pepper
  • Salt (for pasta water only)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Step 1: Cook the Pasta

  1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add spaghetti and cook until al dente.
  2. Reserve ½ cup of pasta water before draining.

Step 2: Prepare the Sauce

  1. While pasta cooks, beat the eggs and yolks in a bowl.
  2. Add Pecorino and a generous amount of freshly ground black pepper. Mix until smooth.

Step 3: Cook the Guanciale

  1. In a large skillet, cook guanciale over medium heat until crispy and golden.
  2. Remove from heat. Add drained pasta directly to the pan with guanciale.

Step 4: Combine

  1. Off the heat, pour the egg mixture over the hot pasta. Toss quickly and vigorously to coat without scrambling.
  2. Add reserved pasta water a little at a time until the sauce becomes silky.

🍽️ Serving Suggestion

Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and cracked black pepper. Pair with a glass of Frascati or Trebbiano.

🧀 Chef’s Tip

Never add cream — the creaminess comes from the eggs and cheese. Use guanciale if possible for the most authentic Roman flavour.

Buon appetito! 🇮🇹
Tried it? Share your Carbonara on Instagram and tag @garlicsoffritto.
entry.summary: A true Roman classic — creamy without cream, rich with egg, pecorino, and guanciale. Carbonara is the soul of simplicity and indulgence.

entry.title: Homemade Penne Pasta (Penne Fatte in Casa)

link.title: Homemade Penne Pasta (Penne Fatte in Casa)

entry.content:
Fresh penne pasta with that perfect bite — made with just flour, eggs, and a bit of love. Once you try it, you’ll never go back.

📝 Ingredients

  • 400g (3¼ cups) semolina flour or 00 flour
  • 4 large eggs
  • Pinch of salt
  • Optional: a splash of olive oil

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Step 1: Make the Dough

  1. On a clean surface or in a large bowl, mound the flour and create a well.
  2. Crack the eggs into the well, add a pinch of salt.
  3. Gradually incorporate flour into the eggs with a fork, then knead into a dough.
  4. Knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Wrap and let rest 30 minutes.

Step 2: Shape the Penne

  1. Roll the dough out into sheets (2–3 mm thick).
  2. Cut into small rectangles, roll each around a wooden dowel or pasta tool to shape into penne tubes.
  3. Let dry on a floured surface or pasta rack.

Alternatively: Use a pasta extruder with a penne die for quicker, uniform results.

🫒 Serving Suggestion

Toss with your favourite sauce — arrabbiata, pesto, or carbonara. Pairs well with a robust Montepulciano d’Abruzzo.

✨ Chef’s Tip

Use semolina for a firmer bite and better sauce grip. Let pasta dry for 15–20 minutes before cooking to help it hold shape.

Buon appetito! 🍝
Making pasta from scratch? Show us your penne magic @garlicsoffritto!
entry.summary: Fresh penne pasta with that perfect bite — made with just flour, eggs, and a bit of love. Once you try it, you’ll never go back.

entry.title: The Best Homemade Lasagne (Lasagna alla Bolognese)

link.title: The Best Homemade Lasagne (Lasagna alla Bolognese)

entry.content:
Layers of rich ragù, creamy béchamel, and silky pasta sheets — this classic Italian lasagne brings the warmth of a Sunday lunch in Emilia-Romagna straight to your kitchen.

📝 Ingredients

For the Ragù (Meat Sauce):

  • 2 tbsp olive oil
  • 1 small onion, finely diced
  • 1 small carrot, finely diced
  • 1 celery stalk, finely diced
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 250g (½ lb) ground beef
  • 250g (½ lb) ground pork
  • 100ml (⅓ cup) dry red wine
  • 400g (14 oz) canned San Marzano tomatoes, crushed
  • 2 tbsp tomato paste
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • A few basil leaves or a bay leaf
  • Optional: a splash of milk (to soften the acidity)

For the Béchamel Sauce:

  • 4 tbsp (60g) unsalted butter
  • 4 tbsp (30g) all-purpose flour
  • 3 cups (700ml) whole milk, warmed
  • Freshly grated nutmeg (just a pinch)
  • Salt to taste

Other:

  • Fresh lasagne sheets (or dried, pre-cooked ones)
  • 100g (1 cup) grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese
  • Butter (for greasing and dotting on top)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Step 1: Make the Ragù

  1. Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed pot.
  2. Add onion, carrot, and celery. Sauté on medium-low until soft (about 8 minutes).
  3. Stir in garlic and cook briefly until fragrant.
  4. Add beef and pork. Brown the meat, breaking it up with a wooden spoon.
  5. Pour in red wine and let it reduce until mostly evaporated.
  6. Add crushed tomatoes and tomato paste. Stir in basil or bay leaf.
  7. Simmer gently, uncovered, for at least 1½ hours. Add a splash of milk toward the end to balance acidity.

Step 2: Prepare the Béchamel

  1. In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat.
  2. Whisk in flour to form a smooth roux. Cook for 1–2 minutes, stirring constantly.
  3. Gradually whisk in the warm milk. Continue whisking until thick and smooth.
  4. Season with salt and a pinch of nutmeg.

Step 3: Assemble the Lasagne

  1. Preheat oven to 180°C / 350°F.
  2. Lightly grease a baking dish with butter.
  3. Start layering: a spoon of ragù, a layer of pasta, ragù, béchamel, a sprinkle of Parmigiano. Repeat for 4–5 layers.
  4. Finish with béchamel and extra Parmigiano on top. Dot with a few small pieces of butter.

Step 4: Bake

  • Cover loosely with foil and bake for 25 minutes.
  • Uncover and bake another 15–20 minutes until bubbling and golden.
  • Let rest for at least 15 minutes before serving.

🍷 Serving Suggestion

Serve with a simple arugula salad and a glass of Chianti Classico or Barbera. Lasagne is even better the next day!

🤌 Chef’s Tip

For an extra-authentic touch, use fresh egg pasta sheets and simmer your ragù for 3+ hours — just like in Bologna.

Buon appetito! 🇮🇹
Have you tried this recipe? Tag us on Instagram @garlicsoffritto and share your results!
entry.summary: Layers of rich ragù, creamy béchamel, and silky pasta sheets — this classic Italian lasagne brings the warmth of a Sunday lunch in Emilia-Romagna straight to your kitchen.

entry.title: Creamy Spinach Risotto (Risotto agli Spinaci)
entry.content:
A creamy, vibrant green risotto made with fresh spinach, arborio rice, and a touch of Parmigiano. Elegant enough for guests, comforting enough for a quiet night in.

📝 Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • 2 tbsp unsalted butter
  • 1 small onion, finely diced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 cup arborio rice
  • 1/3 cup dry white wine
  • 1.2L (5 cups) vegetable or chicken broth, kept warm
  • 150g (5 oz) fresh spinach leaves
  • 30g (¼ cup) grated Parmigiano-Reggiano
  • Zest of ½ lemon (optional, but recommended)
  • Salt and black pepper to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Step 1: Blanch the Spinach

  1. Bring a small pot of water to boil. Add the spinach and cook for 30–40 seconds until wilted.
  2. Drain and immediately plunge into ice water to retain color.
  3. Squeeze out excess water and purée in a blender or food processor until smooth. Set aside.

Step 2: Cook the Risotto

  1. In a large saucepan, heat olive oil and 1 tbsp butter over medium heat.
  2. Add onion and cook until translucent (about 4–5 minutes).
  3. Stir in garlic, then add the rice. Toast it for 1–2 minutes until slightly translucent at the edges.
  4. Pour in white wine and stir until absorbed.
  5. Begin adding the warm broth, one ladle at a time, stirring constantly and allowing each addition to be absorbed before adding more.
  6. Continue until the rice is tender but slightly al dente, about 18–20 minutes.

Step 3: Finish the Dish

  1. Stir in the spinach purée and remaining butter.
  2. Add Parmigiano and lemon zest, stirring to combine.
  3. Season to taste with salt and black pepper.

🍽️ Serving Suggestion

Top with a few shavings of Parmigiano and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil. Pair with a crisp white wine like Verdicchio or Pinot Grigio.

🌿 Chef’s Tip

For an even silkier finish, blend a spoon of mascarpone into the risotto just before serving. It adds richness without overpowering the spinach.

Buon appetito! 🌱
Let us know how your risotto turned out — tag us @garlicsoffritto on Instagram!
entry.summary: A creamy, vibrant green risotto made with fresh spinach, arborio rice, and a touch of Parmigiano. Elegant enough for guests, comforting enough for a quiet night in.
